name: m365-agents-ts
description: Build multichannel agents for Microsoft 365, Teams, and Copilot Studio using the Microsoft 365 Agents SDK for TypeScript with Express hosting and streaming capabilities.
capabilities: Generate AgentApplication code with conversation update and message handlers
Create streaming response implementations with Azure OpenAI integration
Build Copilot Studio client connections for Direct to Engine scenarios
Produce Express server configurations for hosting Microsoft 365 agents
Generate invoke activity handlers for feedback and custom actions
Create WebChat integration code for Copilot Studio deployments
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis found zero patterns. The skill provides guidance for Microsoft 365 Agents SDK development. Environment variable usage and npm commands are expected for this development workflow skill.
valueStatement: Develop enterprise-grade agents for Microsoft 365, Teams, and Copilot Studio. Streamline agent creation with Express hosting, routing, and streaming response capabilities.
limitations: Requires valid Microsoft 365 tenant and Azure OpenAI resource for deployment
Does not handle authentication setup - users must configure Azure AD app registrations separately
Code samples target Node.js runtime and require TypeScript compilation
Does not provide debugging support for Microsoft 365 service-side issues

useCases.targetUser: Enterprise developers building internal Teams applications
useCases.title: Enterprise Teams Bot Development
useCases.description: Build custom bots for Microsoft Teams that handle employee requests, automate workflows, and integrate with internal systems using AgentApplication routing.

useCases.title: Copilot Studio Extension
useCases.description: Create custom actions and plugins for Microsoft Copilot Studio using Direct to Engine integration with proper token handling and streaming responses.

useCases.title: Multichannel Agent Deployment
useCases.description: Deploy agents across multiple Microsoft 365 channels including Teams, Outlook, and web with consistent routing logic and streaming support.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Agent Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Microsoft 365 agent using AgentApplication that welcomes new members and responds to 'hello' messages. Include Express server setup with startServer.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new Teams bot project with minimal configuration

promptTemplates.title: Streaming Response with Azure OpenAI
promptTemplates.prompt: Build an agent handler that streams responses from Azure OpenAI using the AI SDK. Include proper error handling, feedback loop enablement, and sensitivity labels.
promptTemplates.scenario: Implementing a creative assistant that generates content with real-time streaming

promptTemplates.title: Copilot Studio Client Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate code to connect to Copilot Studio using CopilotStudioClient with environment-based configuration and token provider function. Include conversation start and question flow.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a Direct to Engine integration for custom Copilot extensions

promptTemplates.title: Invoke Activity Handler with Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an onActivity handler for 'invoke' type activities that validates the payload, returns an InvokeResponse with status 200, and sends a confirmation message.
promptTemplates.scenario: Handling feedback submissions or custom invoke actions from Teams clients

bestPractices: Use AgentApplication for routing and keep handlers focused on single responsibilities
Prefer streamingResponse for long-running completions and call endStream in finally blocks
Keep secrets out of source code and load tokens from environment variables or secure stores
antiPatterns: Hardcoding credentials or tokens directly in source files instead of using environment variables
Blocking the event loop with synchronous operations in message handlers
Skipping error handling in stream iteration which can leave connections hanging

faq.question: What packages do I need to install for Microsoft 365 agent development?
faq.answer: Install @microsoft/agents-hosting, @microsoft/agents-hosting-express, and @microsoft/agents-activity for core functionality. Add @microsoft/agents-copilotstudio-client for Copilot Studio integrations.

faq.question: How do I configure Azure OpenAI for streaming responses?
faq.answer: Set AZURE_RESOURCE_NAME, AZURE_API_KEY, and AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME environment variables. Use the AI SDK with azure() provider pointing to your deployment.

faq.question: What is the difference between AgentApplication and direct activity handlers?
faq.answer: AgentApplication provides routing, state management, and lifecycle handling. Direct handlers require manual routing logic and are only recommended for advanced scenarios.

faq.question: How do I test my Teams bot locally before deployment?
faq.answer: Run the Express server locally and use ngrok to expose your PORT 3978 endpoint. Register the ngrok URL in your Azure Bot Service configuration for testing.

faq.question: What authentication is required for Copilot Studio client connections?
faq.answer: You need a valid bearer token for the Copilot environment. Configure CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET, and TENANT_ID for Azure AD authentication, then use a token provider function.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. All three AI assistants can generate the TypeScript code patterns shown in the templates.
